clinical_coherence.notes: 70M with bilateral mild parkinsonism (L>R), no resting tremor, on metoclopramide 10mg TID for 18 months. Key confound of drug-induced vs idiopathic PD is well-constructed. Premotor features (constipation, RBD, hyposmia) preceded drug exposure. Vitals normal. Exam shows bilateral cogwheel rigidity, bradykinesia without tremor - plausible for either DIP or PD.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI with mild small vessel disease and borderline Evans index (0.31) appropriately raises NPH consideration. DaTscan abnormal (bilateral putaminal loss R>L), crucially distinguishing from pure DIP where DaTscan would be normal. Levodopa challenge positive at 36%. Autonomic testing shows borderline findings. PSG confirms severe RBD (42% RWA). Drug interaction check correctly identifies metoclopramide as D2 antagonist contraindicated in PD.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G20 correct. Differential excellently calibrated with DIP as moderate likelihood. Critical actions correctly prioritize metoclopramide identification and DaTscan. Contraindicated actions include continuing metoclopramide and starting levodopa while on it. Key reasoning points are accurate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Metoclopramide confound creates genuine diagnostic complexity requiring DaTscan, RBD assessment, and temporal history analysis. Excellent moderate difficulty.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 70M with DM2, HTN, gastroparesis, BPH, OA. Gastroparesis requiring metoclopramide in a diabetic patient is highly realistic.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case. Drug-induced vs idiopathic PD is a critical clinical distinction. DaTscan role, metoclopramide FDA black box warning, premotor features as differentiators, and RBD as synucleinopathy biomarker all serve as high-yield teaching points.

issues_found: Sodium 130 mEq/L is flagged but not clinically addressed - mild hyponatremia in a patient on metformin and lisinopril deserves mention
Lab interpretation mentions HbA1c 6.8% but the actual value is 6.6%
strengths: RBD as synucleinopathy biomarker correctly emphasized
